note: One creative reused across Instagram, Facebook & Google Business Profile. 10 posts to choose from, diversified by format/hook/topic, each modelled on a niche over-performer and rewritten in EHN clinician voice.

posts.visual_recipe: Solid purple background (#6B5B95) with a centered cyan/bright blue butterfly illustration (black outline, symmetrical wings) positioned in upper-third. posts.angle: Why your thyroid symptoms might persist even with "normal" blood tests — and what functional medicine looks at instead.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Instagram carousel (3 slides, square 1080x1350px each).

Slide 1: Solid purple background (#6B5B95). Centered cyan/bright blue thyroid gland illustration (simple line art, symmetrical, black outline) positioned in upper third. White bold sans-serif heading "Your Thyroid Isn't the Problem" (~60pt, centred below illustration). White subheading "Your blood tests might be missing it" (~38pt, centred). Small circular arrow icon bottom right corner. Clean, minimal layout with ample whitespace.

Slide 2: Same purple background (#6B5B95). White bold heading "The TSH-Only Trap" (~50pt, top-left aligned). White body text (~24pt) listing: "Standard tests check TSH only. Functional medicine looks at: Free T3 • Free T4 • Thyroid antibodies • Nutrient status (iron, zinc, selenium)". Small icons or bullets in cyan. Ample whitespace.

Slide 3: Same purple background (#6B5B95). White bold heading "What We Do Differently" (~50pt, top-left aligned). White body text (~24pt): "We run comprehensive thyroid panels. We ask about your actual symptoms — fatigue, temperature sensitivity, hair loss, mood. We address root causes: nutrient gaps, inflammation, stress." Small circular arrow icon bottom right. Clean layout.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: "Your Thyroid Isn't the Problem | Your blood tests might be missing it"

Slide 2: "The TSH-Only Trap | Standard tests check TSH only. Functional medicine looks at: Free T3 • Free T4 • Thyroid antibodies • Nutrient status (iron, zinc, selenium)"

Slide 3: "What We Do Differently | We run comprehensive thyroid panels. We ask about your actual symptoms — fatigue, temperature sensitivity, hair loss, mood. We address root causes: nutrient gaps, inflammation, stress."
posts.caption:
If your thyroid symptoms haven't budged despite "normal" blood tests, you're not alone — and you're not imagining it.

Standard pathology checks TSH only. Functional medicine goes deeper: we look at free T3, free T4, thyroid antibodies, and the nutrient status (iron, zinc, selenium) your thyroid desperately needs.

A "normal" TSH doesn't mean your thyroid is working well *for you*. It means it's sitting in a reference range that was built for a population, not for *your* energy, temperature regulation, mood, and hair growth.

We listen to what your body is actually telling you — then we run the tests that match.

If this sounds familiar, let's talk about what's really going on.

#ThyroidHealth #FunctionalMedicine #ItIsntAllo YourHead #Adelaide
posts.gmb_caption: You're not imagining thyroid symptoms — standard TSH tests often miss the full picture. At Elemental Health & Nutrition, we run comprehensive thyroid panels and listen to your actual symptoms to find the root cause. Book an appointment with our team in Adelaide today.

posts.angle: What mould exposure does to your immune system — and why your home test matters more than you think
posts.build_brief: Format: vertical reel cover (9:16, or short-form video thumbnail). Canvas: clean white background (#FFFFFF) with soft grey accent panel (#F5F5F5) on lower third. Hook text: Large, bold, clean sans-serif (at least 60pt weight), white text on dark teal (#2C5F7F) banner across top 1/4 of frame, reading "IS MOULD HIDING IN YOUR HOME?" — positioned for maximum stop-scroll impact. Visual: Centre frame shows a clinician (EHN team member or diverse healthcare practitioner) holding or gesturing toward a mould test kit (e.g. petri dish or branded swab kit) in a bright, modern home interior — natural light from window, minimal clutter, professional but warm. Palette: clinical clean (whites, soft greys, teal accents), warm daylight, trustworthy + accessible. Feel: functional-medicine credible, not alarmist. Include subtle EHN logo or watermark bottom right.
posts.on_image: Reel hook frame: "IS MOULD HIDING IN YOUR HOME?" (large bold text, top banner) | 2–3 beat script outline: Beat 1 (0–2s): Clinician holds test kit, looks directly at camera with warm curiosity — "Most of my clients don't realise mould exposure can quietly trigger chronic inflammation, brain fog, and respiratory issues." Beat 2 (2–4s): Cut to mould-prone areas (bathroom, under sink, bedroom corner) — "It's not always visible. That's why testing changes everything." Beat 3 (4–6s): Return to clinician, soft smile — "Want to know if mould might be affecting your health? Let's talk about it."
posts.caption:
Is mould silently triggering your symptoms? 🏠

Most people don't connect their persistent brain fog, fatigue, or respiratory niggles to mould exposure—but the research is clear: mycotoxins and spores can activate a chronic inflammatory response, even when you can't see the problem.

The tricky bit? Mould doesn't always announce itself. A home test can tell you *exactly* what you're breathing—and whether it's worth investigating further.

If you've been managing symptoms without real answers, environmental testing might be the missing puzzle piece. We can help you interpret results and design a practical plan.

Curious if mould might be your hidden driver? Let's explore it together.

#FunctionalMedicine #MouldAwareness #EnvironmentalHealth #AdelaideWellness #ChronicInflammation
posts.gmb_caption: Could hidden mould in your home be driving your chronic fatigue, brain fog, or respiratory symptoms? A simple test can reveal what you're breathing—and we can help you understand what to do next. Book a consultation to explore environmental triggers affecting your health.

posts.angle: New EHN guide to fasting protocols for metabolic reset — evidence-based, not dogma.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: square card (1080 × 1080px) — single image, not carousel.

Background: Bright hot-pink (#FF1493) filling the entire canvas for maximum visual impact.

Center-left positioning: Create a 3D-rendered or styled "book cover" mockup (or use a digital product cover template) with white background. The cover should feature:
- Three overhead food photography shots stacked vertically down the left side of the cover (e.g., a bowl of oats, eggs/protein, fresh vegetables — representing whole foods, not supplements). Images should be high-quality, warm-toned, real food styling.
- Bold serif headline: "INTERMITTENT FASTING: A CLINICAL GUIDE" in two colours — primary word in magenta (#FF1493), secondary in deep navy (#001F3F).
- Subline in smaller serif: "Reset your metabolic health with science, not shortcuts."
- Author line in small sans-serif: "Elemental Health & Nutrition Clinic, Adelaide."
- Position this cover mockup center-left on the canvas, allowing white breathing room to the right.

Bottom-right area: White sans-serif announcement text (all-caps, bold): "AVAILABLE NOW" or "FREE DOWNLOAD" (your choice — whichever EHN prefers).

Overall feel: Clean, clinical, product-focused, high-contrast, modern. Warm but credible. Food imagery anchors authenticity; hot-pink draws the scroll-stop energy from the winner without feeling gimmicky.
posts.on_image:

No supplement stack required. Just clear, functional medicine thinking.

If you're managing insulin resistance, energy crashes, or just want to understand your own metabolism better, this is a practical starting point.

#IntermittentFasting #MetabolicHealth #FunctionalMedicine #NutritionClinic #AdelaideHealth

posts.angle: How a small shift in your daily routine can transform your energy and digestion—one clinic story at a time
posts.build_brief: Text-only social card. Single square format (1080 × 1080px). Plain white or soft cream background (#FAFAF8). Headline in warm charcoal (#2C2C2C), 18-20pt, centre-aligned. No graphics, icons, or photos—emoji accents only (🤍💙) used sparingly as punctuation between sentences. Minimal, clean, clinical feel. Font: sans-serif, readable on mobile. Overall aesthetic: conversational warmth without design clutter.
posts.on_image:
How the smallest shifts really do ripple through your whole day 🤍

I had a client in clinic last week—totally exhausted by 3pm, bloated after meals, sleep all over the shop. Nothing dramatic, just... flat. She mentioned she'd been skipping breakfast, grabbing coffee on the go, eating lunch at her desk while working. Sound familiar?

We chatted about what a proper morning rhythm could look like for *her*—not a rigid plan, just slowing down a bit. Gentle movement. Actually sitting to eat. Within two weeks? Different person. Energy stable. Digestion calmer. Sleep deeper.

Sometimes the magic isn't in a big overhaul. It's in noticing what's missing and adding it back in 🤍💙

Worth a think, hey.

posts.visual_recipe: Light beige/cream background with centered explanatory headline (bold grey text, ~65 words) above a scientific line-graph illustration. The graph uses teal/turquoise line for progesterone, red dotted line for estradiol, with labeled lifecycle stages (childhood, teens, reproductive years, perimenopause, menopause) along x-axis. A green box highlights the perimenopause window. Clean, educational aesthetic with credible citation below. No faces or talking heads.
posts.angle: Estrogen dominance myth: why perimenopause progesterone drop is the real driver of mood & inflammation shifts
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Carousel (4 slides, square format optimised for Instagram feed).
Slide 1 (Hook slide): Beige/cream background (#F5F0E8). Centred bold grey headline (#3A3A3A) at top, ~65 words, debunking the "estrogen is the problem" narrative. Simple teal checkmark icon or "myth/fact" symbol top-left corner. Clean sans-serif (Montserrat Bold, 32px). Subline in lighter grey: "Here's what's actually shifting."

Slides 2–3 (Graph + lifecycle): Same beige background. Side-by-side or stacked layout: 
- Left/top: Line-graph illustration showing two lines across lifecycle stages (childhood | teens | reproductive years | perimenopause | menopause). Teal solid line = progesterone (steeper decline mid-cycle to perimenopause). Red dotted line = estradiol (fluctuates wildly in perimenopause, then declines). Green highlight box around "perimenopause window" (approx. 5-8 year span). Y-axis unlabelled but suggests amplitude; x-axis labelled clearly.
- Right/bottom: 3–4 bullet points in grey text explaining: "Progesterone falls first & fastest" / "Estradiol becomes erratic" / "The *ratio* matters more than absolute levels" / "This ratio shift drives: mood changes, sleep disruption, joint aches, histamine sensitivity."

Slide 4 (CTA/Takeaway): Beige background, centred text: headline "What this means for your health" (bold grey, ~28px). 2–3 sentences in body text (#555555): "Rather than fighting oestrogen, functional medicine focuses on supporting progesterone production, detoxification pathways, and metabolic resilience during this transition. If you're experiencing unexplained mood shifts, sleep issues, or inflammatory symptoms in your 40s–50s, it's worth checking the *pattern* of your cycle—not just single-point hormone tests." Subtle EHN logo or watermark bottom-right. Warm, clinical, no emojis or supplement imagery.

Overall palette: Warm-neutral (creams, beiges, soft greys, teal accent). Educational & credible, not sales-y. Clinical diagrams, no lifestyle imagery or faces.
posts.on_image: Slide 1: "MYTH: Oestrogen is the problem at perimenopause. FACT: Progesterone's rapid fall—and oestrogen's wild swings—create the real shift in mood, sleep, and inflammation. The *ratio* matters more than the number." | Slide 2: Graph illustration with two hormone lines across lifecycle, green perimenopause highlight box. Teal line = Progesterone | Red dotted line = Oestradiol | X-axis: Childhood | Teens | Reproductive Years | Perimenopause | Menopause | Slide 3: "Why this ratio shift hits hard: Progesterone = anti-inflammatory, mood-stabilising, sleep-promoting | Oestradiol = fluctuates wildly, amplifying sensitivity to histamine & stress | Result: mood swings, poor sleep, joint aches, skin flares. All reversible with the right support." | Slide 4: "What this means: Rather than fighting oestrogen, we support progesterone production & metabolic resilience. Have you noticed unexplained changes in your 40s–50s? Book a chat with our team."

posts.angle: Walking as a conversation catalyst: why gentle movement unlocks emotional wellbeing for families
posts.on_image: Slide 1 (Hook frame): "WALKING DOES MORE:" (top, white sans-serif) | "Than just move your body" (bottom, white text on solid teal/turquoise background) | Close-up talking head: female clinician in warm earth-tone top, genuine smile, looking thoughtfully downward, soft blurred neutral background, high-production podcast reel feel.
posts.build_brief: Format: vertical reel cover (9:16, single hook frame for Instagram Reel). Background: soft warm neutral blur (cream/grey gradient). Headline placement: two-line text card overlay. Top line "WALKING DOES MORE:" in white, clean sans-serif (e.g. Poppins Bold), ~40px, left-aligned with breathing room. Bottom line "Than just move your body" in white, same font weight (~50px), positioned on a solid teal/turquoise block (#4ECDC1 or similar), taking up lower third of frame. Overall feel: clinical-warm, podcast-professional, inviting not clinical. Ensure clinician's face is framed naturally in upper two-thirds (soft lighting, genuine expression), with microphone or wellness setting visible to signal credibility.
posts.caption:
Who do you want to take a walk with today? 🌿

Walking is one of those deceptively powerful tools we talk about in functional medicine. Yes, it's brilliant for cardiovascular and metabolic health. But what really gets my attention is what happens *between* people when you're moving together.

There's something about the rhythm of walking, the side-by-side angle, the gentle pace — it lowers defences. Kids talk more openly. Partners reconnect. Stress softens. Your nervous system actually settles.

If you're looking to rebuild connection with someone you care about, or help your child express what's really going on — a walk might be the most evidence-backed conversation starter you've got.

Try it this week. Notice what lands differently.

What walks matter most to you? Let me know.

#WellnessWalks #FunctionalMedicine #MindBodyConnection #NervousSystemHealth #AdelaideClinic
posts.gmb_caption: Walking isn't just physical exercise — it's one of the most effective ways to support nervous system health and deepen connection with the people around you. Research shows that gentle, rhythmic movement opens the door to better conversations and emotional wellbeing. Book a consultation with us to explore how movement can be part of your functional medicine plan.

posts.angle: How your nervous system's regulation pattern affects digestion, energy, and mood — and what resets it.
posts.build_brief: Canvas: Square card (1080×1080px, Instagram/Facebook primary). Background: Clean white (#FFFFFF). Top section: Circular headshot photo (280px diameter) of a warm, professional female clinician from EHN team, positioned top-left with name + credentials ("Dr [Name], Functional Medicine Practitioner") in 16pt sans-serif (Montserrat or equivalent), black text, light grey subtitle. Body text: 5 short listicle statements in 32pt bold black sans-serif (one per line, generous line spacing). Each line starts with a verb tied to nervous-system state (e.g. "Racing thoughts…", "Tight stomach…", "Shallow breathing…", "Restless sleep…", "Stuck energy…") followed by a short descriptor. Final line is the thesis in 36pt, slightly heavier weight. Colour palette: black, white, one accent colour (soft sage or warm terracotta, #C9ADA7 or #9B8B7E) used sparingly as underline or background block behind thesis line. Minimal, professional, high-contrast. No icons, no decoration — text-led design emphasizing clarity.
posts.on_image:
Racing thoughts keep you wired after dark.

Tight stomach when stress hits.

Shallow breathing without noticing.

Restless sleep despite being exhausted.

Stuck energy that won't shift.

Your nervous system needs regulation, not willpower.
posts.caption:
Your nervous system isn't broken — it's just been stuck in one gear.

When we're chronically stressed or dysregulated, our body reads every signal as a threat: digestion slows, energy crashes, sleep fragments, and mood gets flat. This isn't a defect. It's an appropriate response to a sustained stressor.

The good news? Nervous-system regulation can be learned and restored. We work with your body's natural ability to shift from fight-or-flight into rest-and-digest — through breathing patterns, movement, vagal tone work, and addressing the root stressors (often food sensitivities, sleep debt, or relational strain) that keep you locked in place.

If you recognise yourself in any of these, let's talk about what regulation actually looks like for your body.

Book a chat with us — we work with the nervous system, not against it.

#NervousSystemHealth #FunctionalMedicine #VagalTone #MentalWellness #AdelaideClinic
posts.gmb_caption: Your nervous system affects everything — digestion, sleep, mood, and energy. If you're stuck in stress mode, we can help you reset. Book an appointment with our functional-medicine team in Adelaide to explore what nervous-system regulation looks like for you.

posts.angle: How consistent sleep patterns are the single most accessible longevity lever most people ignore

posts.on_image:
Slide 1: 
**"Seven hours of consistent sleep adds 10 years to your lifespan."**
Most people chase expensive tests and supplements. They miss the one thing that moves every longevity marker: sleep architecture. | 
Slide 2:
**One week of poor sleep breaks your metabolic regulation.** Glucose control tanks. Inflammation spikes. Your immune system forgets how to work. Yet we treat it like an inconvenience. |
Slide 3:
**Sleep isn't recovery. It's when your body actually lives.** Your glymphatic system clears brain toxins. Your hormones reset. Your cells repair. Nothing—no supplement, no protocol—replaces consistent sleep. *Start with the fundamentals.*
posts.caption: Most people spend thousands on tests and supplements, but the single biggest lever for longevity sits right in front of you: consistent sleep. Seven hours a night, same time every night, isn't luxury—it's the non-negotiable foundation of every system in your body. Your metabolic health, immune resilience, cognitive clarity, even your ability to manage stress: they all run on sleep. When sleep is chaotic, nothing else works properly. This is where functional medicine starts—not with prescriptions or powders, but with the basics done well. If your sleep is all over the place, that's the first thing we work on together. Everything else builds from there. Ready to dial this in? Book a consultation and let's assess your sleep architecture. #FunctionalMedicine #SleepHealth #Longevity #Adelaide #WellnessFromTheGround
posts.gmb_caption: Most longevity strategies miss the obvious: consistent sleep is the foundation of every health marker. Seven hours nightly resets your metabolism, strengthens immunity, and clears brain toxins. If your sleep is erratic, nothing else works as well. We help you build sleep architecture that actually sticks. Book an appointment today to get started.

posts.angle: The paradox of "healthy" processed foods masking inflammatory ingredients — and how to read what's actually in your cart.
posts.on_image: Slide 1 (Hook frame): "You Think It's Just Yoghurt / I Looked at the Label." | Slide 2: "Your supermarket shelf has 47 'low-fat' options. / Only 3 aren't laced with hidden sugars." | Slide 3: "Most people buy the one with the health claim. / Functional medicine asks: what's it actually doing to your gut?"
posts.build_brief: Format: Vertical reel cover + 2-slide carousel (square 1080x1080). Canvas 1: Soft-focus photograph of a hand holding a supermarket yoghurt tub in sharp focus, lower third of frame. Person's face is blurred, neutral/thoughtful expression. White bold sans-serif text (thick stroke, all-caps) overlaid center, reading the label. posts.caption:
You think it's just a yoghurt.

Your GP said probiotics are good. Your mate swears by the "natural" label. So you pick the one with the health claim and move on.

But functional medicine asks a different question: what's it *actually* doing to your system?

Most supermarket yoghurts carry 15–20g of added sugar per serve (hello, dysbiosis). The "low-fat" versions swap fat for refined carbs. "Natural" flavours might include additives that feed inflammatory bacteria.

None of this is your fault — labelling is designed to be confusing. But your gut microbiome doesn't care about marketing. It reads the ingredient list.

If you're managing digestive issues, autoimmune concerns or metabolic health, what you're eating between meals matters as much as what you eat at them.

We're here to help you decode what's really on the shelf — and what actually supports your health.

Keen to chat about what's hiding in your pantry? Let's start there.

#FunctionalMedicine #GutHealth #NutritionMadeSimple #AdelhealthCare #ReadTheLabel
posts.gmb_caption: You think it's just a yoghurt — but the ingredient list tells a different story. Most supermarket versions are packed with hidden sugars and additives that can feed inflammation, not health. At Elemental Health & Nutrition, we help you decode labels and choose foods that actually support your system. Book a consultation to learn what's really in your fridge.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only Facebook post with no image; long-form caption (234 words) structured as hook + stat drop + authority credentials + 4 bolded myth-busting claims with evidence + CTA. Heavy use of arrows (👉) and line breaks for scanability. Hierarchical text formatting emphasizes shocking stats ("3 in 4 dogs," lifespan drop 17→10 years) and expert credibility (40-year vet, 9 books) upfront, followed by numbered contrarian claims (kibble ingredients, spay/neuter timing, neurotoxins, vaccine frequency). No visual elements; relies entirely on typography, white space, and psychological pattern-matching to drive engagement.
posts.angle: Myth-bust the hidden toxin load in everyday Australian homes — functional medicine take on mould, microplastics, and their link to chronic fatigue and inflammation.
posts.build_brief: Text-only graphic for Facebook (single post, no carousel). No image required. Design brief for clarity: white or cream background (#F5F3F0 or #FFFFFF), left-aligned black text (#1A1A1A), mix of regular and bold weights. Use arrow symbols (👉) for visual breaks. Include 1–2 shocking stats in larger/bolder font. Line breaks between sections for scannability. Clean, minimal, clinical feel — think functional medicine whitespace aesthetic, not salesy. No photos, icons, or illustrations. Rely entirely on typography hierarchy, white space, and text formatting (bold, arrows, line breaks) to drive engagement.
posts.on_image:
Your GP probably isn't screening for this...

👉 1 in 3 Australians show signs of mould-related illness. The average person inhales 40+ microplastic particles daily.

Something is very wrong — and it's invisible.

**The myths your doctor won't challenge:**

**→ Mould only matters if you see it.** Mycotoxins (mould's neurotoxins) hide in walls, HVAC systems, and damp areas you can't see. They trigger chronic fatigue, brain fog, and immune dysregulation long before visible growth appears.

**→ Microplastics are just in the ocean.** They're in your drinking water, tap water, sea salt, and gut. New research links them to systemic inflammation and barrier dysfunction — the root of most modern chronic illness.

**→ Your home's humidity level doesn't matter.** Anything above 55% RH creates ideal conditions for mould and dust mite proliferation. Most Australian homes sit at 60–75% without intervention.

**→ A standard allergy test will catch environmental toxins.** Mycotoxin exposure and microplastic load require functional medicine investigation — blood work, environmental assessment, and provocation testing your standard GP won't order.

The pathway is always the same: exposure → immune activation → chronic inflammation → disease.

Ready to investigate what's really driving your symptoms? Let's talk.

Warm regards,
EHN
posts.caption:
Your GP probably isn't screening for this...

1 in 3 Australians show signs of mould-related illness. The average person inhales 40+ microplastic particles daily. Something is very wrong — and it's invisible.

Functional medicine starts by asking: *what's in your environment?* Mycotoxins, microplastics, and hidden moisture aren't exotic problems — they're hiding in most Australian homes and triggering chronic fatigue, brain fog, and immune dysregulation long before a diagnosis appears.

Standard allergy tests won't catch them. Your GP won't screen for them. But a functional medicine lens will — through environmental assessment, provocation testing, and a deeper investigation into what's driving your symptoms.

If you've been tired, foggy, or inflamed for years without answers, environmental toxin exposure might be the missing piece.

Let's investigate what's really going on. Book a consultation with us — we're here to help you find the root cause.

#FunctionalMedicine #MouldIllness #MicroplasticsAwareness #ChronicFatigue #ToxinExposure #AdelaideClinic #WellnessInvestigation
